examples.title: Two-Phase Locking Implementation
examples.description: Basic pseudocode showing how 2PL manages locks for transactions
examples.code:
// Two-Phase Locking Protocol
BEGIN TRANSACTION
 // Growing Phase
 LOCK_EXCLUSIVE(item_X)
 LOCK_SHARED(item_Y)
 // Perform operations
 READ(item_X)
 WRITE(item_X, new_value)
 // Shrinking Phase - cannot acquire new locks after this
 UNLOCK(item_X)
 UNLOCK(item_Y)
END TRANSACTION

// Lock Manager Functions
FUNCTION acquire_lock(transaction_id, item, lock_type)
 IF lock_compatible(item, lock_type)
 GRANT_LOCK(transaction_id, item)
 ELSE
 ADD_TO_WAIT_QUEUE(transaction_id, item)
 TRANSACTION_WAIT()
 END IF
examples.explanation: Demonstrates basic 2PL where locks are acquired in growing phase and released in shrinking phase. The lock manager handles compatibility checks and queuing.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for lock acquisition with proper indexing
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of locked items

examples.title: Timestamp Ordering Protocol
examples.description: Pseudocode for basic timestamp ordering concurrency control
examples.explanation: Shows how timestamp ordering enforces serializability by comparing transaction timestamps with data item timestamps. Includes Thomas Write Rule optimization.

examples.title: Wait-Die Deadlock Prevention
examples.description: Pseudocode showing Wait-Die scheme for preventing deadlocks
examples.explanation: Demonstrates how Wait-Die prevents deadlocks by always having younger transactions abort when they conflict with older ones holding locks.

examples.title: Optimistic Concurrency Control Validation
examples.description: Pseudocode showing OCC validation phase with read and write sets
examples.code:
// Optimistic Concurrency Control - Validation Phase

FUNCTION VALIDATE_TRANSACTION(T)
 // Read Set: items read during execution
 // Write Set: items to be written
 
 FOR each transaction Ti in COMMITTED_or_ACTIVE
 IF Ti == T
 CONTINUE
 END IF
 
 // Check for write-read conflict
 // Ti wrote something T read
 FOR each X in T.read_set
 IF X in Ti.write_set
 IF Ti commits before T validation
 ABORT_TRANSACTION(T)
 RETURN FALSE
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Check for write-write conflict
 // Ti wrote something T will write
 FOR each X in T.write_set
 IF X in Ti.write_set OR X in Ti.read_set
 IF Ti commits before T validation
 ABORT_TRANSACTION(T)
 RETURN FALSE
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR
 END FOR
 
 RETURN TRUE

// If validation succeeds, perform write phase
examples.explanation: Shows how OCC validates transactions by checking for conflicts with other transactions' read and write sets before committing.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of active transactions
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m is size of read/write sets

examples.title: Two-Phase Commit Protocol
examples.description: Pseudocode for distributed transaction commit protocol
examples.code:
// Two-Phase Commit Protocol

// Coordinator Process
FUNCTION TWO_PHASE_COMMIT(transaction T)
 // Phase 1: Prepare
 FOR each participant P in T.participants
 SEND_PREPARE(P)
 END FOR
 
 received_prepare = RECEIVE_ALL_RESPONSES()
 
 IF ALL(prepared) THEN
 // Phase 2: Commit
 FOR each participant P
 SEND_COMMIT(P)
 END FOR
 LOG_COMMIT(T)
 RETURN COMMIT_SUCCESS
 ELSE
 // Abort
 FOR each participant P
 SEND_ABORT(P)
 END FOR
 LOG_ABORT(T)
 RETURN ABORT
 END IF

// Participant Process
FUNCTION HANDLE_PREPARE(T)
 IF CAN_PREPARE(T) THEN
 WRITE_PREPARE_LOG(T)
 SEND_VOTE_PREPARED(coordinator)
 ELSE
 SEND_VOTE_ABORT(coordinator)
 END IF

FUNCTION HANDLE_COMMIT(T)
 WRITE_COMMIT_LOG(T)
 EXECUTE_CHANGES(T)
 SEND_ACK(coordinator)

FUNCTION HANDLE_ABORT(T)
 UNDO_CHANGES(T)
 SEND_ACK(coordinator)
examples.explanation: Shows the two-phase commit process where coordinator first ensures all participants can commit (prepare phase), then sends commit or abort decisions.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of participants
